commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh In that day a man shall cast away his idols of silver, and his idols of gold, which they made, each for himself to worship, to the moles and to the bats; Bats. The Egyptians adored all sorts of animals. (Herodotus ii. ) Ægyptus portenta colat. (Juvenal xv. ) Omnigenumque Deum monstra. (Virgil, Æneid viii. ) The mole was much esteemed by magicians, who promised any the art of divination and success, who should eat the heart of one still warm. (Pliny, xxx. ) The Israelites were always ready to embrace such superstitious practices.
